_comment: HOW TO FILL THIS OUT — every possible setting is listed below, so you can see the full shape at a glance.
Copy this file to config/goals.json and edit that copy (goals.json is gitignored, so your real
goals never get committed). Every setting can also be overridden per-run with a CLI flag
(--any, --min-profit, --want, etc.) — see README.md for the full flag list.
IMPORTANT: JSON has no 'nil' — its equivalent is 'null'. Any setting below set to null means
'off / not used'. Leaving a setting at null is completely safe; it just means that particular
filter doesn't apply. You don't have to fill in every value — just the ones you care about.
=== THE ONE SETTING THAT OVERRIDES ALL THE OTHERS ===
"any": true  ->  ignore every filter below completely. Just show every trade you can
physically make with your inventory (matches one or more owned items),
ranked best-first. Use this first to sanity-check the tool is finding
anything at all before you start narrowing with filters.
"any": false ->  (default) apply the filters below normally.
=== VALUE SOURCE ===
"value_basis": which site's numbers to price trades by.
One of: "gamersberg", "fruityblox", "bloxfruit", "bloxfruitsvalues".
Default "gamersberg". If your chosen source has no value for a given item, the tool
automatically falls back to whichever other source does have a number for it — you
don't need to change this unless you specifically distrust one source.
=== PROFIT / VALUE FILTERS (all null = off) ===
"min_profit": minimum value gained, in raw value units. Example: 10000000 means the trade
must net you at least +10,000,000 value. null = no minimum.
"min_profit_pct": minimum profit as a fraction. Example: 0.1 means +10% or better. null = no minimum.
"min_get_value": minimum total value of what you'd receive, regardless of profit. null = no minimum.
"max_give_value": maximum total value of what you'd have to give up. null = no maximum.
"any_fair": true = also allow trades with zero or positive delta even with no profit filter set
(i.e. "don't lose value", not necessarily "gain" value). false = don't add this allowance.
=== DEMAND / ITEM FILTERS (null/false = off) ===
"min_demand": minimum demand, 0-10 scale, of the item(s) you'd receive. Higher = easier to
re-trade later. null = no minimum.
"want_item": only show trades where you'd receive one of these exact item name(s).
Can be a single name ("Kitsune") or a list (["Kitsune", "Yeti"]) to match ANY one of
them (not all at once). null = don't require any specific item.
"want_item_include_permanent": true (default) = wanting "Kitsune" also matches a trade
giving "Permanent Kitsune" — assumes you'd be happy with either version. false = only
match the exact name(s) in want_item.
"exclude_lose_wfl": true = skip trades where the community's 'Lose' votes outnumber
'Win'+'Fair' votes combined. false = don't filter on this.
=== TRUST / FRESHNESS / OWNERSHIP LIMITS (all null = off) ===
"min_confidence": minimum trust score, 0-100. Confidence blends value-source agreement,
listing freshness, community vote volume, and whether the game's 40% Beli rule could even
be verified for this trade — see GOALS.md. null = no minimum, but 50+ filters out a lot of
noise from suspicious/unverifiable trades (e.g. common fruit for an inflated Limited item).
"max_age_hours": drop listings posted more than this many hours ago. Example: 24 = last day
only. null = no age limit.
"max_qty_per_fruit": block a trade if you'd end up owning more than this many of any single
fruit it gives you (checked per fruit independently — 3 means up to 3 of EACH fruit, not a
shared budget of 3 total). Example: with 1, a trade offering you a Dough is blocked if you
already own 1 Dough. null = no cap.
=== OUTPUT ===
"limit": max number of ranked results to write out (highest-scoring trades first).
